name: Masa Biscuits
recipeIngredient: 4 ounces Unsalted butter, well-chilled, cut into small pieces
1/2 cup Solid vegetable shortening, well-chilled, cut into small pieces
recipeInstructions:
1. Position racks in the upper and middle thirds of the oven and preheat the oven to 450°F.
2. In a large bowl, stir together 3 3/4 cups of the flour, the masa, baking powder, and salt.
3. With a pastry cutter, blend in the butter and shortening until the mixture resembles a coarse and slightly lumpy dough.
4. Stir in the buttermilk until a soft, crumbly dough is formed.
5. Sprinkle half of the remaining flour on the work surface. Turn the dough out, gather it into a ball, and briefly knead it just until it holds together.
6. Flatten the dough, sprinkle with the rest of the flour, and roll it out to about 1-inch thickness.
7. Using a round 3-inch cutter, cut out biscuits and transfer them to ungreased baking sheets, spacing them about 2 inches apart.
8. Gather scraps into a ball, roll it out to 1-inch thick, and cut out the remaining biscuits.
9. Set the baking sheets on the racks and bake for about 15 minutes, exchanging positions of the sheets from top to bottom and front to back at the halfway point, or until the biscuits are golden and crisp.
10. Serve hot or warm. Note: Masa is NOT the same as cornmeal. Stores catering to Hispanic populations will have masa. Since masa contains no gluten, these biscuits are especially tender.
